title: Please update OSShinji
wikitext:
[]: Please update OS.Shinji             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Nov 14, 2013 1:51 pm With all of the interesting Evangelion sentience conversations going on, I must ask the question: why does Shinji need to be the pilot? Hell, why to the Evas even need pilots at all? We've demonstrated that they're sentient and that they can move on their own. It shouldn't even be important that the pilot is a son/daughter, since it's been demonstrated that Evangelions can move without pilots. Is Shinji just there for the sake of "lol Shinji", or what? Even if he were to act as a controller for the Evangelion, we've seen that Gendo was pretty damn happy when Yui went on a rampage in the second episode. What's going on here?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Nov 14, 2013 1:54 pm There's one time when an Eva moves without a pilot and it's a relatively small movement and the arm just freezes in place. The Evas may be fully conscious at all times (I will accept that for the sake of argument that they are but I still don't agree) but they clearly can't move whenever they please due to some kind of control system that can be overridden. The pilots are reasonably sane and can be conversed with and work according to a plan. The Evas themselves seem to have no interest other than "smash in that particular direction". One of them tries to kill itself and smash everything for no readily apparent reason the first time it's turned on.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Nov 14, 2013 1:55 pm Chuckman wrote: There's one time when an Eva moves without a pilot and it's a relatively small movement and the arm just freezes in place.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 14, 2013 1:57 pm Because letting Eva Units move on their own volition is a tremendously fucking terrible idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Nov 14, 2013 1:59 pm Of course it's a bad idea to let an Evangelion off of its leash! However, Gendo seemed pretty happy about it in the second episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Nov 14, 2013 1:59 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Did you forget EoE, mate? The Eva can move without a pilot. It can even move when the pilot is incapacitated and doing jack shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 14, 2013 2:07 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Of course it's a bad idea to let an Evangelion off of its leash! However, Gendo seemed pretty happy about it in the second episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Nov 14, 2013 2:11 pm Isn't Gendo's excitement more about Yui assuming control than the Evas being able to move on their own?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 14, 2013 2:27 pm Chuckman wrote: Isn't Gendo's excitement more about Yui assuming control than the Evas being able to move on their own?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Nov 14, 2013 2:41 pm Addressing the issue of Shinji being Yui's child, does that have any correlation to him being able to pilot it? I'm starting to doubt it at this point. EDIT: Stoopid Nuke-kun is stupid. I'll think about this more next time; the answer to my own question is in my post following Reichu's.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 14, 2013 3:32 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Addressing the issue of Shinji being Yui's child, does that have any correlation to him being able to pilot it? I'm starting to doubt it at this point.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Nov 14, 2013 4:52 pm I absolutely remember it, and it was the core of all of my Evangelion related beliefs until yesterday. Maybe it's saying that the only people that can 'control' restricted Evangelions are the children. That would make more sense then them being the conduit that requires them to move.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 14, 2013 4:58 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Maybe it's saying that the only people that can 'control' restricted Evangelions are the children. That would make more sense then them being the conduit that requires them to move.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Nov 14, 2013 5:01 pm Well, we know that the Evas were fully sentient for the entire show. However, in Yui's case, she wasn't able to prance through the tulips and eat Gendo for breakfast because of the inhibitors you described earlier. Assuming that she became fully autonomous after ingesting the S2 engine, a pilot would become obsolete. However, before that point, something/someone would be needed to overcome the inhibitors and control the Evangelion. That somebody would be Shinji, overcoming the inhibitors via the A-10 nerve and being able to since he was Yui's son. In short, Shinji is what allows the Evangelion to function normally, but also controls its functionality.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Tue Nov 19, 2013 5:27 am They don't have the ability to awaken the Evas at will. Eva-01 only ever moves on her own to protect Shinji, which is obviously a calculated outcome on Gendo's part. I assume their consciousness is more or less dormant whenever they aren't going berserk.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Tue Nov 19, 2013 5:54 am Unit-01 moving without a pilot in the very first episode is the most confusing moment in NGE, for me, because everyone's relatively blasé about it. Compare the reaction the same Eva going berserk gets in the next episode, for example. I'm just thankful that they reference it again (episode 20), and they actually afford it the gravity it deserves.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Nov 19, 2013 6:49 am Chuckman wrote: If you mean Yui breaking out of the bakelite, then yeah, okay. Still, that's a single explosive movement and not a coordinated action. Also, AuraTwilight made my point in fewer words. Letting an Eva move freely is like giving a nuclear bomb the power to decide when it goes off.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Nov 19, 2013 10:59 am It's one of those movies that I need to see but never have. I grew up in the era of VHS. Sometimes I forget that you can find anything in five minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 19, 2013 11:03 am Atropos wrote: Unit-01 moving without a pilot in the very first episode is the most confusing moment in NGE, for me, because everyone's relatively blasé about it.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Nov 19, 2013 11:10 am Chuckman wrote: I grew up in the era of VHS.

Hell, why to the Evas even need pilots at all? We've demonstrated that they're sentient and that they can move on their own. It shouldn't even be important that the pilot is a son/daughter, since it
